title: Integrative Procedure Evaluation
abstract: Procedural knowledge must be learned, an often subtle and difficult process—but it must also be enacted. Procedure enaction is not as tricky a topic as procedure learning, but still is far from trivial, and involves the real-time interaction of procedures, during the course of execution, with other knowledge. In this brief chapter we explain how this process may be most naturally and flexibly carried out, in the context of CogPrime’s representation of procedures as programs (“Combo trees”).
